response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a capable full-stack and systems engineer with a broad polyglot background, but he is a poor fit for this role. His C++ exposure is minimal — limited to a 6-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor thesis project — falling far short of the mandatory 5–10 years. His primary stack is TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang, with no evidence of Windows platform development. Crucially, he has no demonstrated experience in capital markets, fixed income, investment banking, or any adjacent financial-services domain. His brief time at RIDE Capital involves retail investment tooling in Germany, not institutional capital markets systems.
strengths: Strong communication skills evidenced by multiple public workshops and conference talks
CI/CD and DevOps familiarity across multiple roles, including GitLab, Docker, and trunk-based development
Java development background from robotic-arm and mobile-tooling work at Altom Consulting
concerns: C++ experience is superficial — roughly 6 months of professional use in 2014–2015, not the 5–10 years required
No Windows platform development; career is predominantly Linux and web-based environments
Zero exposure to fixed income, fixed income derivatives, investment banking, or capital markets systems
